text.title: Research Workflow Specifications
text.summary: Step-by-step specifications for Idea Discovery, Product Research, and Project Naming workflows.

section.title: Workflow: Idea Discovery
section.p: Sequential workflow for generating and validating startup or product ideas. Output: idea-validation.md.

list.item: Step 1 — Gather context: skills, interests, resources (AskUserQuestion)
Step 2 — Generate 5-10 ideas using 7 Ps framework and other methods (mode: ideas)
Step 3 — User selects 3-5 ideas to research (AskUserQuestion, multiSelect)
Step 4 — Pain point research: Reddit, forums, reviews mining (mode: pains)
Step 5 — Niche evaluation: market size, competition, barriers (mode: niche)
Step 6 — User selects idea or loops back to Step 2 (max 3 iterations)

rule.statement: Context questions must cover three dimensions: skills/experience, motivation (solve own problem / market opportunity / passion / side income), and available time investment.
rule.rationale: Ideas generated without this context are generic and unlikely to match the founder's actual constraints, leading to selection of ideas they cannot execute.

section.title: Workflow: Product Research
section.p: Comprehensive research workflow for existing product concepts. Output: executive-summary.md plus per-module files.

list.item: Step 3 — Ask user for modules and mode (quick/deep)
Step 4 — Run selected research agents sequentially (not parallel)
Step 5 — Write executive-summary.md with TL;DR, top-3 risks, top-3 opportunities, recommended next step

rule.statement: Run faion-marketing-manager before faion-sdd when go-to-market unknowns dominate; run faion-sdd first when feasibility is the larger risk. Do not treat them as parallel options.
rule.rationale: The two downstream consumers have different inputs. Marketing-manager needs market data; sdd needs technical feasibility. The order determines which blockers get resolved first.

section.title: Workflow: Project Naming
section.p: Generate and validate project or product names with domain availability. Output: update to constitution.md.

list.item: Step 2 — Generate 20 candidates using naming strategies (mode: names)
Step 3 — User selects favorites or requests more (AskUserQuestion, multiSelect)
Step 4 — Filter to 5 with cheap whois/HEAD checks, then run faion-domain-checker-agent on top 5 only
Step 5 — User selects final name or loops back to Step 2 (max 3 iterations)
Output — Update constitution.md with selected name

rule.statement: Run cheap availability checks (whois, HTTP HEAD) on all 20 candidates first; call faion-domain-checker-agent only on the top 5. Do not run the full domain checker on all 20.
rule.rationale: Running the full domain checker (Namecheap API, registrar calls) on 20 candidates burns quota. Two-pass filtering reduces API spend by 75% with negligible accuracy loss.

section.reference: Bash script for batch domain and handle availability checks
